name: Cauliflower with Beef "Sichuan Style
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Lan chi black bean paste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut steak into 1-inch cubes.
2. Make mushroom paste by pureeing the mushrooms.
Combine soy and sherry for marinade. Marinate steak pieces for 1 hour, massaging to aid tenderizing. Drain and reserve marinade.
3. Wash and soak mushrooms for 1 hour; drain and reserve soaking liquid. Thinly slice mushrooms.
4. Wash, peel, and roll-cut carrots (slicing on a slight bias into 1-inch lengths but turning the carrot 90 degrees between slices).
5. Wash cauliflower, trim stems, and cut into florets about the size of carrot pieces.
6. Parboil carrots in boiling stock for 3 minutes or until just beginning to soften. Reserve carrots and stock.
Stir-frying:
7. Heat peanut oil in a hot wok. When oil begins to smoke, add steak cubes; stir-fry quickly for 1 minute until meat loses pinkness. Do not overcook. Remove to plate.
8. Add ginger, garlic, and peppercorns; stir-fry 15 seconds.
9. Add cauliflower and mushrooms; stir-fry 1 minute.
10. Add reserved carrot stock, marinade, and mushroom soaking liquid. Bring to boil. Cover wok; reduce heat to medium and simmer for 3 minutes.
11. Remove lid; turn heat to high. When sauce boils again, add carrots and beef. Mix together.
12. Splash vinegar on sides of wok. Push ingredients from sauce, thicken with cornstarch paste. Sauce should be a light gravy. Add sesame oil. Toss in green onion. Serve.
